source_hierarchy_note: The 2025 Version 2.5 standards remain canonical for wording, numbering, and level progression. 2014/2016 materials are supplemental evidence only.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Builds a cumulative noun vocabulary, then uses previously learned nouns for contextual translation and multiple-meaning choices.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 3-7 / Nouns
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: noun vocabulary foundations
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Defines nouns, introduces gender and plural forms, and drills basic identification before Chumash transfer.
supplemental_matches.notes: Supports the 2025 noun-vocabulary progression with instructional explanations rather than cumulative counts.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 22-28 and 36 / Vocabulary Appendix Standards 3.1-3.2; Sample List of Nouns Per Grade
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: high-frequency noun banks and grade sequencing
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides noun-frequency lists plus a sample noun list by grade for cumulative vocabulary planning.
supplemental_matches.notes: Useful for vocabulary scope and sequence, but tabular extraction and Hebrew lists still require manual confirmation.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 73-76 / 3.1 Vocabulary: שמות עצם (nouns)
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: noun-vocabulary assessment patterns
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Shows contextual translation, out-of-context translation, grouping, and multiple-meaning assessment patterns for taught noun vocabulary.
supplemental_matches.notes: Supports later assessment design, not canonical wording.

crosswalk_records.notes: All three supplemental sources reinforce the 2025 noun-vocabulary strand, with the appendix supplying frequency evidence and the sample-assessment source supplying item shapes.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Builds cumulative root vocabulary for verbs, then identifies weak-letter and altered-root patterns and chooses contextual translations.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 35-40 / Verbs; Roots - שרשים; Present and Future Tense Verbs
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: verb roots and weak-letter awareness
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Explains what a shoresh is, connects roots to translation, and introduces complete versus weak roots with tense changes.
supplemental_matches.notes: Strong instructional support for the 2025 verb-root progression.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 22-27 and 37 / Vocabulary Appendix Standards 3.1-3.2; Sample שורשים List
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: high-frequency verb roots and grade examples
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides frequency-ranked Hebrew verbs and a sample shoresh list aligned to Standards 3.1-3.2.
supplemental_matches.notes: Useful evidence for root-vocabulary sequencing; Hebrew lists require human verification.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 79-87 / 3.2 Vocabulary: שרשים (verbs)
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: shoresh identification and contextual translation assessment
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Shows direct shoresh identification, grouping of words that share a root, and contextual root-translation questions.
supplemental_matches.notes: Provides strong item-model evidence for future draft diagnostics.

crosswalk_records.notes: The older sources strongly support the 2025 shoresh strand and clarify how cumulative verb-root knowledge may be exercised.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Builds a cumulative bank of function words and chooses contextual meanings for words with multiple uses.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 16-20 / Conjunctions, Prepositions and Other Grammatical Items; Definite Article; Prepositions
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: function-word and prefix vocabulary
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Introduces conjunctions, prepositions, article behavior, and attached prefix forms that students must translate accurately.
supplemental_matches.notes: Covers much of the function-word strand, though the broadest adverb/question-word coverage is clearer in the sample-assessment source.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 32-35 / III. Hebrew Particles; Prepositions and Prepositional Phrases
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: particles and high-frequency function words
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Supplies particle, preposition, and phrase lists that extend the function-word inventory behind Standard 3.03.
supplemental_matches.notes: Table-heavy material needs human review, but it clearly supports the vocabulary bank.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Page 88 / 3.3 Other vocabulary: prepositions, conjunctions, adverbs, numbers, questioning words
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: explicit Standard 3.3 scope confirmation
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Confirms that older Standards 3.3 assessment design grouped prepositions, conjunctions, adverbs, numbers, and question words together.
supplemental_matches.notes: Important historical evidence for how the broad “Other Vocabulary” bucket was interpreted.

crosswalk_records.notes: The supplemental sources support the broad function-word strand, but the underlying tables and OCR still need manual review before any fine-grained machine extraction.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Moves from identifying nouns and adjectives in learned words to recognizing gender, number, irregular forms, and סמיכות in unlearned פסוקים.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 3-11 and 48 / Nouns; Adjectives; construct-chain note in ניקוד section
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: noun/adjective recognition, agreement, and smichut cues
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Defines nouns and adjectives, drills gender and number matching, and notes how construct-chain forms can affect endings and ניקוד.
supplemental_matches.notes: The construct-chain references are present but should be checked against the PDF before downstream reuse.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Page 38 / Appendix for Parts of Speech, 3.4 B and C
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: gender and number heuristics for nouns
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides explicit masculine/feminine and singular/plural heuristics, including irregular endings and exceptions.
supplemental_matches.notes: Especially helpful for the 2025 expectation around irregular-looking noun forms.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 89-100 / 3.4 Nouns and Adjectives
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: parts-of-speech assessment architecture
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Shows assessment prompts for noun identification, gender/number distinctions, adjective recognition, adjective agreement, and סמיכות recognition.
supplemental_matches.notes: Provides practical evidence for how the strand was operationalized in assessment form.

crosswalk_records.notes: This is one of the strongest crosswalked strands because all three supplemental sources reinforce the same practical skills.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Progresses from basic English and Hebrew pronoun categories to identifying referents, suffixes, and embedded pronouns in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 23-30 / Pronouns; Subjective and Objective Pronouns; Possessive Nouns and Pronouns
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: pronoun categories, referents, and suffixes
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Builds from basic pronoun replacement and referents into Hebrew pronoun types and possessive suffixes in Chumash.
supplemental_matches.notes: Strong instructional support for the 2025 pronoun progression.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 39-44 / Pronoun appendices for Standards 3.5A-H
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: pronoun reference charts and suffix families
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides subject, possessive, objective, demonstrative, prepositional, and interrogative pronoun reference material.
supplemental_matches.notes: Chart content is especially useful for future teacher-reviewed validation layers.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 101-121 / 3.5 Pronouns
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: pronoun referent and suffix assessment patterns
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Shows referent identification, person/gender/number distinctions, possessive and objective pronouns, demonstratives, and pronominal suffix interpretation.
supplemental_matches.notes: Clear evidence that the older framework already assessed pronouns in layered steps.

crosswalk_records.notes: Pronouns have exceptionally strong supplemental evidence across instructional, appendix, and assessment sources.

crosswalk_records.canonical_standard_title: Prepositions, Conjunctions and the Definite Article
crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Builds from direct recognition of conjunctions and articles to distinguishing multiple prefix functions, article types, and the two meanings of את in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 16-20 / Conjunctions, Prepositions and Other Grammatical Items; Definite Article; Prepositions
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: conjunctions, articles, and inseparable prefixes
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Explains conjunctive and reversing ו, the definite article, prepositional prefixes, and related ניקוד changes.
supplemental_matches.notes: The 2014 intro organizes this material slightly differently, but it clearly supports the 2025 substandard.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 46-55 / article rules, ו ההיפוך, and Appendix to 3.6G “The Word את”
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: article/prefix rule details and the two functions of את
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Adds rule charts for the definite article, inseparable prefixes, vav consecutive, and a teacher-facing explanation of את as “with” versus direct-object marker.
supplemental_matches.notes: This is strong supporting evidence, but the dense Hebrew examples and charts require manual checking.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Page 88 / 3.3 Other vocabulary
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: historical bucket for prepositions and conjunctions
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Shows that earlier assessment design grouped much of this material under “Other Vocabulary” rather than a separate later substandard.
supplemental_matches.notes: Useful for organization history, not for overriding 2025 numbering or scope.

crosswalk_records.notes: The supplemental sources strongly support the content strand, even though the older assessment source organizes some of it under 3.3 rather than the later 2025 substandard split.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Moves from identifying verbs and simple tense markers to translating verbs across person, tense, active/passive forms, and binyan families.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 35-43 / Verbs; Present, Future, and Past Tense; root changes
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: verb recognition, tense markers, prefixes, suffixes, and weak roots
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Introduces verbs, tense shifts, איתן markers, present/future/past formation, and irregular root behavior.
supplemental_matches.notes: Matches the 2025 strand closely at the rule-sequence level.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 22-27 and 50-53 / high-frequency verbs; ו ההיפוך rule material
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: verb banks and tense-sensitive vav behavior
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides frequency-ranked verb vocabulary and applied grammar notes about how vav consecutive affects verb tense reading.
supplemental_matches.notes: Helpful as extension material, but some later appendix pages extract poorly and should be verified against the PDF.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 79-87 / 3.2 Vocabulary: שרשים (verbs)
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: root-centered verb assessment examples
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Supports the translation and grouping side of verb-root knowledge, but not the full later 2025 verb-parsing range.
supplemental_matches.notes: Useful for early verb assessment shape, not a full replacement for the 2025 verb strand.

crosswalk_records.notes: The supplemental evidence is strong for foundational verb work, though the full 2025 binyan/passive range remains more expansive than the older extracted support.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Groups a פסוק into meaningful phrases and adjusts Hebrew word order when translating into English.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Page 9 / Adjectives and Hebrew-to-English word order
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: why Hebrew word order cannot always be translated literally
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Explicitly explains that Hebrew adjective order differs from English and that direct word-for-word order can mislead translation.
supplemental_matches.notes: Clarifies the translation-order problem but does not supply a full dedicated grouping curriculum.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 97-100 / 3.4 adjective matching and סמיכות examples
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: phrase-level relationship identification
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides assessment examples that require distinguishing adjective-noun relationships from construct chains, which indirectly supports grouping skills.
supplemental_matches.notes: Helpful for syntax adjacency, but still not a one-to-one dedicated match for 3.08.

crosswalk_records.notes: The supplemental materials clarify the need for syntax-aware translation, but the later 2025 grouping/word-order strand is more explicit than the older sources.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: Uses טעמי המקרא to divide, punctuate, and translate a פסוק appropriately.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 13-15 / Trop
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: taamim as punctuation and clause-division signals
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Explains that taamim guide chanting, stress, and punctuation, with etnachta and other pauses dividing the pasuk into meaning units.
supplemental_matches.notes: A strong instructional foundation for parsing the pasuk by taamim.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 57-59 / Appendix to Standard 3.9 “Punctuation in a פסוק”
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: explicit 3.9 parsing appendix
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Provides a teacher-facing appendix mapping punctuation concepts to taamim-based pasuk parsing, including etnachta and comma-like breaks.
supplemental_matches.notes: This is the clearest direct supplemental match to 2025 Standard 3.09.

crosswalk_records.notes: Standard 3.09 has strong direct support from both the instructional guide and an appendix dedicated specifically to this strand.

crosswalk_records.canonical_student_expectation_summary: For advanced students, explains the systems of תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and the interaction between ניקוד and טעמי המקרא.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 47-53 / Vowels, sheva, dagesh, syllables, and related ניקוד rules
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: advanced ניקוד rule sequence
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Introduces vowel families, sheva categories, syllable division, gutturals, and dagesh behavior in a rule-teaching format.
supplemental_matches.notes: This is the strongest older source for the 2025 advanced-nikud strand.

supplemental_matches.supplemental_section_or_page: Pages 46-51 / article ניקוד charts, prefix vocalization, and ו ההיפוך summaries
supplemental_matches.matched_topic: applied ניקוד charts for grammar rules
supplemental_matches.matched_text_summary: Supplies chart-style applied ניקוד examples tied to articles, inseparable prefixes, and vav behavior.
supplemental_matches.notes: Supports applied ניקוד reading but not the entire advanced rule inventory on its own.

crosswalk_records.notes: The 2014 intro provides robust support for the advanced ניקוד strand, while the appendix supplies applied chart examples that still need table verification.
